skill.name: laravel-expert
skill.description: Senior Laravel engineering guidance for building, refactoring, reviewing, and optimizing production Laravel applications.
skill.summary: Apply modern Laravel patterns for maintainable, secure, and performant backend code.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by normal Laravel guidance text, such as request validation, API design, legacy avoidance, and response structure. No executable scripts, prompt injection, data exfiltration, suspicious networking, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: HTTP Client Library Static Hit Is Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 81 warns not to use request()->all() blindly. This is Laravel validation guidance, not a network client call or data transfer behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a defensive coding rule about Laravel request input handling. There is no external endpoint, HTTP library usage, credential access, or executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines mention security standards, API design, HTTP status codes, and output sections. They do not recommend weak hashing or cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content contains no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, insecure random generation, or cryptographic implementation instructions. The surrounding text promotes secure Laravel practices.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe framework modernization, architecture style, database query practice, view hygiene, anti-patterns, and abstraction boundaries. They do not instruct the model to enumerate host files, users, processes, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is prose guidance for Laravel development and review. No system commands, filesystem traversal, secret discovery, or host reconnaissance instructions are present.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Hit Is Benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 30 lists performance awareness as an engineering priority. It does not include network scanning, host discovery, port probing, or external service enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line is a harmless quality attribute in a principles list. There is no network command, target address, or instruction to probe infrastructure.

content.value_statement: Laravel teams need consistent architecture, validation, and review standards. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward idiomatic Laravel solutions.
actual_capabilities: Designs Laravel features with controllers, services, requests, resources, and policies.
Reviews Laravel code for structure, validation, authorization, performance, and maintainability.
Suggests Laravel-native refactors for controllers, routes, services, models, and views.
Guides API responses, pagination, rate limiting, and HTTP status usage.
Recommends Eloquent patterns that reduce N+1 queries and unsafe mass assignment.
Applies Laravel authentication, authorization, queue, caching, and Blade conventions.
limitations: It focuses on Laravel projects and is not a general PHP framework guide.
It does not inspect running applications unless the host tool provides project access.
It avoids third-party packages unless the user explicitly requests them.
It gives engineering guidance but does not replace security testing or production review.

use_cases.title: Plan a New API Feature
use_cases.description: Create a Laravel-native feature plan with validation, authorization, resources, and database considerations.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Controllers
use_cases.description: Move business logic into clearer services and requests while keeping behavior understandable.

use_cases.title: Review Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Check Laravel code for risky validation, authorization gaps, query inefficiencies, and maintainability issues.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Laravel Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Laravel feature for [feature]. Include routes, controller actions, validation, authorization, and response structure.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Laravel Controller
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Laravel controller for validation, authorization, Eloquent usage, and controller size. Suggest practical improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Service Boundary
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Laravel workflow into clean services, FormRequest validation, policies, and API resources. Explain each tradeoff.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production API Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production Laravel API module for [domain]. Cover architecture, file structure, transactions, queues, caching, tests, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Review my Laravel user update endpoint.
output_examples.output: The response would identify missing authorization, unsafe input handling, possible mass assignment, and opportunities to use FormRequest and API Resources.

output_examples.input: Design an orders API in Laravel.
output_examples.output: The response would outline routes, controller responsibilities, request validation, policies, service methods, resources, transactions, queues, and test coverage.
The response would separate validation, authorization, business logic, persistence, and presentation into Laravel-native components.

best_practices: Provide project context, Laravel version, and relevant files before asking for implementation details.
Ask for validation, authorization, database, and response behavior together for feature work.
Request tests or review criteria when changes affect production workflows.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for non-Laravel PHP, frontend-only, or unrelated infrastructure tasks.
Asking for large rewrites without current code, constraints, or expected behavior.
Accepting generated code without running tests, review, and framework-specific checks.

faq.question: What Laravel versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets modern Laravel projects, especially Laravel 10 and 11 or newer.

faq.question: Can it generate complete Laravel feature examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline routes, controllers, requests, services, resources, policies, and tests.

faq.question: Does it prefer packages or Laravel-native features?
faq.answer: It prefers Laravel-native features unless you ask for a specific package or integration.

faq.question: Can it review existing Laravel code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review structure, validation, authorization, Eloquent usage, and maintainability.

faq.question: Does it run security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides secure coding guidance but does not replace dedicated security testing.

faq.question: Can it help with API design?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers resources, status codes, pagination, rate limiting, validation, and response consistency.
